content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the CockroachDB cluster and the certs available.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- ls -la /tmp/certs/ 2>&1
echo "---CA cert info---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/ca.crt -noout -subject -enddate -fingerprint -sha256 2>&1
echo "---current node cert (from pod mount) info---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- cat /cockroach/cockroach-certs/node.crt 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -subject -enddate -fingerprint -sha256 2>&1
echo "---SANs on current node cert---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- cat /cockroach/cockroach-certs/node.crt 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -text 2>/dev/null | grep -A2 "Subject Alternative Name"

kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1

content.text: Now I'll generate new node and client certs (valid ~365 days) signed by the existing CA, using the same SAN config.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs &&
openssl genrsa -out node.new.key 2048 &&
openssl req -new -key node.new.key -out node.new.csr -config node.cnf &&
openssl x509 -req -in node.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extfile node.cnf -extensions v3_req &&
echo "--- node cert generated ---" &&
openssl x509 -in node.new.crt -noout -subject -enddate -fingerprint -sha256
'

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs &&
openssl req -in client.root.csr -noout -subject -text 2>&1 | head -30
'

content.text: Simple client cert with no SAN extension needed. I'll generate the new client.root cert similarly.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs &&
openssl genrsa -out client.root.new.key 2048 &&
openssl req -new -key client.root.new.key -out client.root.new.csr -subj "/CN=root" &&
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 \
  -extfile <(printf "keyUsage=critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment\nextendedKeyUsage=clientAuth\n") &&
echo "--- client cert generated ---" &&
openssl x509 -in client.root.new.crt -noout -subject -enddate
'

input.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/crdb-certs && cd /tmp/crdb-certs && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs/ca.crt ./ca.crt && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs/node.new.crt ./node.crt && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs/node.new.key ./node.key && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs/client.root.new.crt ./client.root.crt && \
kubectl -n cockroachdb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs/client.root.new.key ./client.root.key && \
ls -la /tmp/crdb-certs

content.text: Files copied locally. Now recreate the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret (it currently doesn't exist per the earlier check).

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs 2>&1
echo "---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/ca.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.key \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs

content.text: Secret created. Now I need to restart the CockroachDB pods so they pick up the new certs (the current pods have the old certs cached in their tmpfs mount from before the secret was deleted).

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset crdb-cluster --timeout=180s

description:
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset crdb-cluster --timeout=180s
